message:
feat(python): chebder and chebint wrapper parity

Completes the Chebyshev-basis calculus triad by wrapping the two kwarg-
heavy helpers alongside the earlier chebadd/chebsub/chebmul/chebval/
chebroots landing:

- chebder(c, m=1, scl=1, axis=0): m-th derivative of a Chebyshev series
  along the given axis, with an optional scaling factor applied at
  every iteration for linear change-of-variable.
- chebint(c, m=1, k=None, lbnd=0, scl=1, axis=0): m-fold antiderivative
  wiring through k (integration constants), lbnd (lower integration
  bound used to pin each antiderivative's constant), scl (per-step
  rescale), and axis.

A single live-NumPy test exercises the kwarg surface: chebder at
m=0/1/2 and with scl=2; chebint default; chebint m=2; chebint with k
scalar-broadcast via length-1 list; chebint m=2 with matching-length
k=[5, 10]; chebint with non-zero lbnd; chebint with scl=2; chebint
with every kwarg specified simultaneously; plus a chebder(chebint(c))
round-trip parity check.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
